title: sagraea pusilliflora
intro: Sagraea pusilliflora, commonly known as Small-flowered Sagraea, is a plant species native to tropical regions. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, potentially aiding in wound healing and reducing fever. The therapeutic actions of Sagraea pusilliflora are attributed to its ability to modulate the immune system and exhibit antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents present in the plant include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects.
side_effects: Sagraea pusilliflora can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its toxic alkaloid content. In excessive doses, it can cause dizziness, headaches, and even seizures, particularly in individuals with pre-existing medical conditions. Prolonged exposure to its toxins can also lead to liver and kidney damage, necessitating immediate medical attention. Furthermore, improper usage can exacerbate existing allergies, resulting in anaphylactic reactions, which require emergency treatment.
